examples.title: Algorithm: Sending SMS Message in Android
examples.description: Demonstrates programmatic SMS sending with permission checks
examples.code:
ALGORITHM SendSMS
BEGIN
 1. Check if SEND_SMS permission is granted
 2. Get default SmsManager instance
 3. Send message using smsManager.sendTextMessage
 - Parameters: destinationAddress, scAddress, text, sentIntent, deliveryIntent
END
examples.explanation:
1. Mandatory permission check for Android API 23+
2. SmsManager handles cellular network communication
3. sentIntent/deliveryIntent track message status

examples.title: Algorithm: Receiving SMS Messages via BroadcastReceiver
examples.description: Implementation pattern for incoming SMS handling
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ReceiveSMS
BEGIN
 1. Create BroadcastReceiver subclass
 2. Register receiver for SMS_RECEIVED action
 3. In onReceive:
 a. Extract pdus from intent extras
 b. Convert each pdu to SmsMessage
 c. Process message content
END
examples.explanation:
2. Requires RECEIVE_SMS permission in manifest
3a. pdus are protocol data units carrying SMS data
3b. SmsMessage.createFromPdu() parses binary data

examples.title: Algorithm: Requesting Location Updates in Android
examples.description: Pattern for continuous GPS location tracking
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GetLocationUpdates
BEGIN
 1. Check ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION permission
 2. Get LocationManager system service
 3. Request location updates using requestLocationUpdates
 - Provider: GPS_PROVIDER
 - Parameters: minTime, minDistance, LocationListener
 4. Implement LocationListener callbacks:
 a. onLocationChanged: Handle new location
END
examples.explanation:
3. minTime: Minimum time interval between updates (ms)
 minDistance: Minimum displacement between updates (meters)
4a. Location object contains latitude, longitude, accuracy

examples.title: Algorithm: Calculate Distance Between Coordinates
examples.description: Haversine formula implementation for LBS applications
examples.code:
ALGORITHM HaversineDistance
BEGIN
 1. Convert latitude/longitude to radians
 2. Compute differences:
 Δlat = lat2 - lat1
 Δlon = lon2 - lon1
 3. Apply formula:
 a = sin²(Δlat/2) + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin²(Δlon/2)
 c = 2 * atan2(√a, √(1−a))
 distance = R * c // R = Earth radius (6371 km)
 4. Return distance
END
examples.explanation:
1. Math functions typically use radians
3. Formula calculates great-circle distance
 Accounts for Earth's curvature
